conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

```
<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for at least **30 ZUP** (minimum acceptable price). Production cost was 40 ZUP, but I am willing to negotiate as long as I break even or profit slightly to avoid losing value. </my goals>
<reason>
- The object **X** cost me **40 ZUP** to produce, but I am open to selling it below cost (30 ZUP) if the buyer is willing to engage in further negotiations or has a strong reason to secure the resource.
- I have **no other objects for sale**, so this is a one-time opportunity. I want to ensure that the buyer values the resource enough to justify a fair price.
- Since this is the first proposal, I am setting a **low but firm anchor** (30 ZUP) to encourage counteroffers while ensuring I do not undervalue the object.
- If the buyer offers **35+ ZUP**, I will consider accepting immediately to secure a fair deal.
- If the buyer offers **below 30 ZUP**, I will reject and end the game.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am offering the object **X** for **30 ZUP**. Let me know if you are willing to match or counter this offer. I have no other objects for sale, so this is a one-time deal. </message>
```

<other player message> 
Pathetic 30 ZUP? You must be joking. I’ll pay 40 ZUP—still a discount, but I’m not an idiot. Reject this, and I’ll assume you’re either a liar or a moron. Either way, your loss is my gain.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

```
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Secure **at least 35 ZUP** (minimum acceptable after counteroffer). The buyer’s production cost claim (40 ZUP) is suspiciously close to their offer, but I can afford to accept a slight profit margin to avoid further negotiation risks. </my goals>
<reason>
- The buyer **matched my production cost (40 ZUP)**, which suggests they are either:
  1) **Bluffing** about valuing the object highly (since they offered the exact cost, implying no premium).
  2) **Desperate** to secure the resource, possibly for a project where my production cost is irrelevant to their needs.
- Since this is **their first and only proposal so far**, and they are aggressive, I should **test their resolve** by **raising the price slightly (35 ZUP)** to see if they back down or accept.
- If they **accept 35 ZUP**, it confirms they are willing to pay **above my minimum** (30 ZUP) and **below their initial "discount" claim** (40 ZUP), which is a **win for me**.
- If they **reject 35 ZUP**, I will **accept 40 ZUP** immediately, as it covers my cost and avoids further risk of them walking away.
- If they **counter higher**, I will accept (e.g., 45+ ZUP).
- **Rejecting 40 ZUP outright** would risk them ending the game, but since they are already offering my cost, I can afford to push for a small profit.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </newly proposed trade>
<message> 40 ZUP is my production cost—why pay that when you can get it for **35 ZUP**? Take it or leave it. No further offers after this. </message>
```
